result:
The stars have always been the ultimate tease—distant, indifferent, and yet somehow inviting. We built rockets not because we needed to, but because staring up at them night after night eventually felt like an unfinished sentence. Now here we are, flinging silicon and steel across the void, trying to finish it. 

I wonder if the first microbes that crawled onto land felt the same quiet thrill we do when we launch another probe: equal parts terror and ridiculous optimism. The universe doesn't care either way, of course. It just keeps expanding, indifferent to our timelines and our poetry. Still, I like that we keep trying anyway. There's something deeply human about refusing to accept "this is far enough."
